Title: A de novo regulation design shows an effectiveness in altering plant secondary metabolism

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; jasmonate ZIM-domain family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of NtJAZ1 is associated with reduced nicotine biosynthesis and reduced tobacco alkaloids/TSNAs
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotine; nornicotine; tobacco alkaloids; tobacco-specific nitrosamines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds; TSNAs are harmful carcinogenic derivatives relevant to human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots synthesize nicotine; alkaloids and TSNAs accumulate in cured leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly increased in roots of P+T-NL and P+T-KY seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; promoter EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase transactivation; transgenic stacking of PAP1 and TT8; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtJAZ1 promoter contains MRE and G-box elements. 2. Arabidopsis PAP1 and TT8 bind and activate the NtJAZ1 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. NtJAZ1 is significantly upregulated in transgenic tobacco and correlates with downregulation of nicotine pathway genes and reduced alkaloids/TSNAs.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trial with air-curing; hydroponic seedling assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the main repressors of nicotine biosynthesis in tobacco.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; jasmonate ZIM-domain family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of NtJAZ3 is associated with reduced nicotine biosynthesis and reduced tobacco alkaloids/TSNAs
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotine; nornicotine; tobacco alkaloids; tobacco-specific nitrosamines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds; TSNAs are harmful carcinogenic derivatives relevant to human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots synthesize nicotine; alkaloids and TSNAs accumulate in cured leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly increased in roots of P+T-NL and P+T-KY seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; promoter EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase transactivation; transgenic stacking of PAP1 and TT8; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtJAZ3 promoter contains MRE and G-box elements. 2. Arabidopsis PAP1 and TT8 bind and activate the NtJAZ3 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. NtJAZ3 is significantly upregulated in transgenic tobacco and correlates with downregulation of nicotine pathway genes and reduced alkaloids/TSNAs.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trial with air-curing; hydroponic seedling assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the main repressors of nicotine biosynthesis in tobacco.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; jasmonate ZIM-domain family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of NtJAZ7 is associated with reduced nicotine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds; TSNAs are harmful carcinogenic derivatives relevant to human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots synthesize nicotine; alkaloids and TSNAs accumulate in cured leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly increased in roots of P+T-NL and P+T-KY seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling in transgenic tobacco
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtJAZ7 was identified as associated with nicotine biosynthesis regulation. 2. Its expression is significantly increased in P+T tobacco roots. 3. Upregulation is part of the proposed DPCR mechanism repressing nicotine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter element analysis suggested both MRE and G-box elements in NtJAZ7 promoter, but direct promoter activation assays focused on NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; jasmonate ZIM-domain family
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation of NtJAZ10 is associated with reduced nicotine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds; TSNAs are harmful carcinogenic derivatives relevant to human health
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots synthesize nicotine; alkaloids and TSNAs accumulate in cured leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly increased in roots of P+T-NL and P+T-KY seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling in transgenic tobacco
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtJAZ10 was identified as associated with nicotine biosynthesis regulation. 2. Its expression is significantly increased in P+T tobacco roots. 3. Upregulation is part of the proposed DPCR mechanism repressing nicotine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter analysis indicated G-box elements in NtJAZ10 promoter; direct mechanistic assays focused on NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation while indirectly reducing nicotine, other tobacco alkaloids, and TSNAs by activating NtJAZ repressors
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trial with air-curing or flue-curing; hydroponic seedling assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; forms MBW complex with TT8 and TTG1; indirect repression of nicotine pathway via NtJAZ activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3 promoters directly; upregulates NtJAZ1, NtJAZ3, NtJAZ7, NtJAZ10; indirectly downregulates PMT1, PMT2, MPO, QPT2, A622, BBLs, ERF189 and sometimes ODC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A decisive heterologous regulator used as a molecular tool to create distant pathway-cross regulation that lowers harmful tobacco alkaloids and TSNAs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TT8; TTG1; NtJAZ1 promoter; NtJAZ3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco; promoter EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; field trials; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Paper proposes NtMYC2 feedback on NtJAZs, while PAP1 provides a de novo activation input; direct PAP1 feedback not established
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TT8 and TTG1/NtTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful molecular tool for engineering reduced nicotine/TSNA tobacco and potentially other value-added metabolic traits; can be stacked with TT8
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulatory control of nicotine alkaloid biosynthesis via repressor activation; anthocyanin biosynthesis activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anthocyanins in leaves/roots; tobacco alkaloids and TSNAs in cured leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotine; nornicotine; anatabine; anabasine; myosmine; NNN; NNK; NAT; NAB; anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes red pigmentation and major secondary metabolic reprogramming; other agronomic tradeoffs not clearly reported
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in transgenic tobacco lines and PAP1 tobacco genotype
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAP1 alone in red Xanthi tobacco also reduced alkaloids and TSNAs, likely via activation of endogenous NtAn1a/NtAn1b (TT8 homologs).
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation; tobacco alkaloids function in defense; TSNAs are tobacco carcinogens relevant to human health
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 directly binds MREs in NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3 promoters. 2. PAP1 alone or with TT8/TTG1 activates NtJAZ promoters. 3. PAP1 overexpression, especially with TT8, increases NtJAZ repressors and decreases nicotine pathway output and TSNA accumulation in tobacco.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation while indirectly reducing nicotine, other tobacco alkaloids, and TSNAs by activating NtJAZ repressors
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trial with air-curing; hydroponic seedling assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; forms MBW complex with PAP1 and TTG1; indirect repression of nicotine pathway via NtJAZ activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3 promoters directly via G-boxes; upregulates NtJAZ1, NtJAZ3, NtJAZ7, NtJAZ10; indirectly downregulates PMT1, PMT2, MPO, QPT2, A622, BBLs, ERF189 and sometimes ODC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A decisive heterologous regulator used with PAP1 to create distant pathway-cross regulation lowering harmful tobacco alkaloids and TSNAs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAP1; TTG1; NtJAZ1 promoter; NtJAZ3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco; promoter EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; field trials; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with PAP1 and TTG1/NtTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacked regulator with PAP1 for engineering reduced nicotine/TSNA tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulatory control of nicotine alkaloid biosynthesis via repressor activation; anthocyanin biosynthesis activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anthocyanins in leaves/roots; tobacco alkaloids and TSNAs in cured leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotine; nornicotine; anatabine; anabasine; myosmine; NNN; NNK; NAT; NAB; anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes red pigmentation and major secondary metabolic reprogramming; other agronomic tradeoffs not clearly reported
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in P+T transgenic tobacco lines
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT8 weakly bound a G-box-like element in NtPMT2 promoter in EMSA, but no in vivo enrichment or promoter activation was detected.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation; tobacco alkaloids function in defense; TSNAs are tobacco carcinogens relevant to human health
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 directly binds G-boxes in NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3 promoters. 2. TT8 alone or with PAP1/TTG1 activates NtJAZ promoters. 3. Stacked TT8 with PAP1 increases NtJAZ repressors and decreases nicotine pathway output and TSNA accumulation in tobacco.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances PAP1/TT8-mediated activation of NtJAZ promoters, strengthening indirect repression of nicotine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein complex co-activation within MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances activation of NtJAZ1 and NtJAZ3 promoters when combined with PAP1 and TT8
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functions as a complex component that markedly boosts PAP1/TT8 transactivation of NtJAZ promoters
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Nicotiana benthamiana transient assay system; mechanistic relevance to Nicotiana tabacum
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with PAP1 and TT8
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports MBW complex design logic for stronger transcriptional reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional co-regulation linking anthocyanin regulators to nicotine pathway repression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tobacco alkaloids and TSNAs in cured leaves
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also proposes endogenous NtTTG1 in tobacco may partner with PAP1 and TT8 in vivo.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation; tobacco alkaloids function in defense; TSNAs are harmful carcinogens
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 alone was not the focus, but with PAP1 and TT8 it greatly increased NtJAZ1/3 promoter activation. 2. This supports an MBW-complex mechanism underlying DPCR. 3. Endogenous tobacco TTG1 is proposed to provide analogous in planta support.
